findings.description: Hierarchical table headers with merged cells were flattened.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, header cells for the years (e.g., '1997-1998') span across two sub-columns ('Collection amount' and '% of total'). The HTML conversion removes these merged cells (colspan) and instead concatenates the text into single, verbose column headers like 'Fiscal Year 1997-1998 Collection amount [$]'.

findings.description: Significant loss of inline text formatting (bold and underline) in the footnote section.
findings.evidence: The PDF's footnote text at the bottom of the page relies heavily on bolding and underlining to emphasize dates (e.g., 'May 1, 1999', 'September 1, 2009') and sub-headings (e.g., '+Department of Commerce transfer (§ 105-113.81A):'). All of this inline formatting has been stripped in the HTML rendering, presenting it as plain text.
